summary: The reproduction confirms that the FileUrlValidator allows any URL when allowedFileUrlDomains includes wildcard '*', enabling SSRF attacks on internal services and cloud metadata endpoints.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should confirm that file URLs are not validated when the default wildcard configuration is used, allowing SSRF attacks.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the Parse Server repository, built the Docker image, and verified the vulnerable validation logic in FileUrlValidator.js. The code at line 15-17 shows that when domains.includes('*'), validation returns immediately without checking the URL, confirming the SSRF vulnerability.
verification_method: The verify.sh script searches for the wildcard bypass pattern in FileUrlValidator.js and confirms the default configuration allows any URL. The check identifies the specific vulnerable code path that enables SSRF.
evidence_produced_by_run: File src/FileUrlValidator.js contains vulnerable validation logic at lines 14-17
Code checks if domains.includes('*') and returns without validation
Default configuration in Definitions.js allows wildcard domains
Attacker can create objects with file URLs pointing to arbitrary endpoints
No protection against cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254)

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the alpha branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
